title: salix nuttallii var. capreoides
intro: Salix nuttallii var. capreoides, also known as dwarf river willow or dwarf willow, is a variety of the willow plant that grows in wetland areas and is characterized by its small size and distinctive leaves. The medicinal properties of Salix nuttallii var. capreoides include its ability to reduce inflammation and relieve pain due to its high content of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate headaches, and soothe menstrual cramps, making it a popular choice in herbal medicine. Salix nuttallii var. capreoides is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, and is typically harvested in the spring and summer months.
